title: Specialist doctors flee public hospitals as NHI looms
description: NATIONAL NEWS - If the department of health...
bodyText: [{"type":"paragraph","text":"NATIONAL NEWS - If the department of health does not create a conducive working environment, specialist doctors and other practitioners will keep on moving to the private sector, say experts.","html":"NATIONAL NEWS - If the department of health does not create a conducive working environment, specialist doctors and other practitioners will keep on moving to the private sector, say experts."},{"type":"blockquote","text":"And the exodus could affect the National Health Insurance (NHI), medical experts have warned."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"The experts were commenting on recent reports that specialist doctors were continuing to resign from the government hospitals to join the private sector.","html":"The experts were commenting on recent reports that specialist doctors were continuing to resign from the government hospitals to join the private sector."},{"type":"blockquote","text":"Health department lost 12k doctors"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Statistics from the national department of health revealed that between 2013 and this year, it lost about 12  745 doctors.","html":"Statistics from the national department of health revealed that between 2013 and this year, it lost about 12  745 doctors."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"It further revealed 58 897 nurses, 1  341 pharmacists and 23 941 administrative personnel resigned during the same period.","html":"It further revealed 58 897 nurses, 1  341 pharmacists and 23 941 administrative personnel resigned during the same period."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Health expert Dr Atiya Mosam said the escalating resignation of specialist doctors from the public health sector was a warning of a system under strain, driven by frozen posts, austerity measures and increasingly heavier workloads for junior staff and specialists that remain in the system.","html":"Health expert Dr Atiya Mosam said the escalating resignation of specialist doctors from the public health sector was a warning of a system under strain, driven by frozen posts, austerity measures and increasingly heavier workloads for junior staff and specialists that remain in the system."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Mosam said this threatens the quality of care, worsens inequities between the public and private sectors and undermines clinical supervision and specialist training.","html":"Mosam said this threatens the quality of care, worsens inequities between the public and private sectors and undermines clinical supervision and specialist training."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"“The NHI depends on a functional public health system with adequate specialist capacity to support referral pathways, clinical governance and continuity of care, and this crisis underscores the urgent need to strengthen primary health care (PHC) as weaknesses in PHC means that many patients are reaching specialists for conditions that should have been prevented, detected earlier, or managed at PHC level, unnecessarily overburdening specialist services,” said Mosam.","html":"“The NHI depends on a functional public health system with adequate specialist capacity to support referral pathways, clinical governance and continuity of care, and this crisis underscores the urgent need to strengthen primary health care (PHC) as weaknesses in PHC means that many patients are reaching specialists for conditions that should have been prevented, detected earlier, or managed at PHC level, unnecessarily overburdening specialist services,” said Mosam."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"“This will reduce avoidable hospital demand, allowing specialists to focus on complex care. Therefore, we need decisive action to retain specialists while simultaneously investing in PHC.”","html":"“This will reduce avoidable hospital demand, allowing specialists to focus on complex care. Therefore, we need decisive action to retain specialists while simultaneously investing in PHC.”"},{"type":"blockquote","text":"Specialist doctor exodus not isolated incident"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Mosam said the NHI was still in the making, hence the impact on doctors and what they decide to do was difficult to predict.","html":"Mosam said the NHI was still in the making, hence the impact on doctors and what they decide to do was difficult to predict."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"SA Medical Association (Sama) CEO Dr Mzulungile Nodikida said the recent departure of multiple cardiologists and ear, nose and throat specialists from Chris Hani Baragwanath Hospital was not an isolated incident.","html":"SA Medical Association (Sama) CEO Dr Mzulungile Nodikida said the recent departure of multiple cardiologists and ear, nose and throat specialists from Chris Hani Baragwanath Hospital was not an isolated incident."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Nodikida said the exodus reflected a deepening systemic crisis on a national level in the public health system that has been allowed to persist for far too long, with devastating consequences for patient care, training platforms, and the long-term sustainability of specialist services.","html":"Nodikida said the exodus reflected a deepening systemic crisis on a national level in the public health system that has been allowed to persist for far too long, with devastating consequences for patient care, training platforms, and the long-term sustainability of specialist services."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"“Sama is unequivocal that this exodus is driven by chronic failures in workforce planning and management. Doctors continue to face intolerable working conditions characterised by severe staff shortages, unfilled funded posts, crumbling infrastructure, excessive workloads, delayed or disputed overtime payments, and remuneration that has steadily declined in real terms.”","html":"“Sama is unequivocal that this exodus is driven by chronic failures in workforce planning and management. Doctors continue to face intolerable working conditions characterised by severe staff shortages, unfilled funded posts, crumbling infrastructure, excessive workloads, delayed or disputed overtime payments, and remuneration that has steadily declined in real terms.”"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"“These conditions have eroded morale and made retention of highly skilled professionals increasingly impossible, despite repeated warnings from the profession.”","html":"“These conditions have eroded morale and made retention of highly skilled professionals increasingly impossible, despite repeated warnings from the profession.”"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Nodikida has called for urgent and decisive action from both provincial and national health authorities.","html":"Nodikida has called for urgent and decisive action from both provincial and national health authorities."},{"type":"blockquote","text":"Immediately fill critical specialist posts – Sama"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"The organisation stated that the action required from the government must include the immediate filling of critical specialist posts, stabilisation of employment conditions, strict adherence to commuted overtime agreements and genuine engagement with health care professionals to develop a credible and sustainable retention strategy.","html":"The organisation stated that the action required from the government must include the immediate filling of critical specialist posts, stabilisation of employment conditions, strict adherence to commuted overtime agreements and genuine engagement with health care professionals to develop a credible and sustainable retention strategy."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"“Failure to act decisively will further weaken the public health system and place the health and lives of South Africans, particularly the most vulnerable, at unacceptable risk.”","html":"“Failure to act decisively will further weaken the public health system and place the health and lives of South Africans, particularly the most vulnerable, at unacceptable risk.”"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Nodikida said the ongoing exodus of specialists from the public health sector poses a serious and direct threat to the implementation of the NHI.","html":"Nodikida said the ongoing exodus of specialists from the public health sector poses a serious and direct threat to the implementation of the NHI."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"According to him, NHI was fundamentally dependent on a stable, adequately staffed and skilled health care workforce to deliver services equitably across the system.","html":"According to him, NHI was fundamentally dependent on a stable, adequately staffed and skilled health care workforce to deliver services equitably across the system."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"He added that when experienced specialists leave state hospitals, service capacity is weakened, waiting times increase and clinical outcomes deteriorate, all of which undermine the very objectives of NHI.","html":"He added that when experienced specialists leave state hospitals, service capacity is weakened, waiting times increase and clinical outcomes deteriorate, all of which undermine the very objectives of NHI."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"“A financing framework, no matter how ambitious, cannot succeed in the absence of the human resources required to translate policy into care at the facility level.","html":"“A financing framework, no matter how ambitious, cannot succeed in the absence of the human resources required to translate policy into care at the facility level."},{"type":"blockquote","text":"‘Financing framework cannot succeed in absence of human resources’"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"“This trend also exposes a critical sequencing problem in NHI implementation. Large-scale health system reform cannot be built on an unstable foundation.","html":"“This trend also exposes a critical sequencing problem in NHI implementation. Large-scale health system reform cannot be built on an unstable foundation."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"“The current inability to retain specialists signals unresolved governance, management and working-condition failures that must be addressed before expanding purchasing, contracting and accreditation under NHI","html":"“The current inability to retain specialists signals unresolved governance, management and working-condition failures that must be addressed before expanding purchasing, contracting and accreditation under NHI"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"“Without stabilising the workforce and restoring confidence among health care professionals, NHI risks becoming an administrative exercise that promises universal access on paper while falling short in practice.","html":"“Without stabilising the workforce and restoring confidence among health care professionals, NHI risks becoming an administrative exercise that promises universal access on paper while falling short in practice."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"“Furthermore, the loss of specialists from academic and tertiary hospitals undermines training platforms that are essential for producing the next generation of doctors needed under NHI.","html":"“Furthermore, the loss of specialists from academic and tertiary hospitals undermines training platforms that are essential for producing the next generation of doctors needed under NHI."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"“If training capacity continues to erode, South Africa will face an even deeper skills shortage in the medium to long term, making universal coverage increasingly difficult to achieve. In this context, retaining health care professionals is not optional or secondary to NHI; it is a prerequisite for its success,” he added.","html":"“If training capacity continues to erode, South Africa will face an even deeper skills shortage in the medium to long term, making universal coverage increasingly difficult to achieve. In this context, retaining health care professionals is not optional or secondary to NHI; it is a prerequisite for its success,” he added."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Dr Jack Bloom, the DA’s spokesperson for health in Gauteng, said the loss of specialists is devastating to the health service.","html":"Dr Jack Bloom, the DA’s spokesperson for health in Gauteng, said the loss of specialists is devastating to the health service."},{"type":"blockquote","text":"Devastating to health service"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"“This is due to appalling mismanagement and corruption in all health departments, except the Western Cape. It is yet another reason why the NHI will fail unless it is drastically revised.”","html":"“This is due to appalling mismanagement and corruption in all health departments, except the Western Cape. It is yet another reason why the NHI will fail unless it is drastically revised.”"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Bloom said the NHI was unlikely to happen soon, as there was no funding for it. He, however, said if it was implemented, many doctors would emigrate as they would not want to work in an inefficient and corrupt system.","html":"Bloom said the NHI was unlikely to happen soon, as there was no funding for it. He, however, said if it was implemented, many doctors would emigrate as they would not want to work in an inefficient and corrupt system."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Positive Women’s Network health expert Lindiwe Mahlangu said the exit of specialists can lead to understaffing, overcrowded hospitals and disparities in access to quality medical care.","html":"Positive Women’s Network health expert Lindiwe Mahlangu said the exit of specialists can lead to understaffing, overcrowded hospitals and disparities in access to quality medical care."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"“This brain drain can pose challenges to the effective implementation of NHI, as there may be a shortage of skilled medical professionals to support the system. This can lead to increased pressure on the remaining health care workers, affecting the quality of care provided to patients.”","html":"“This brain drain can pose challenges to the effective implementation of NHI, as there may be a shortage of skilled medical professionals to support the system. This can lead to increased pressure on the remaining health care workers, affecting the quality of care provided to patients.”"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Mahlangu said to address the challenges, the government needs to improve the working conditions before the implementation of the NHI.","html":"Mahlangu said to address the challenges, the government needs to improve the working conditions before the implementation of the NHI."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Article: Caxton publication, The Citizen","html":"Article: Caxton publication, The Citizen"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’","html":"‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’"}]

images.alt: Specialist doctors flee public hospitals as NHI looms
